commit.message:
Add project README with features and setup instructions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix /mcp root routing — normalize scope path for all-tools fallback

The "all" manager received the raw scope with path="/mcp" instead of "/"
because the BaseHTTPMiddleware doesn't strip the Mount prefix. The sub-path
routes (core/dns/docs) already normalized this but the fallback branch didn't.
Also removes debug logging from mcp_router now that routing is verified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add verify-setup.sh + /doctor for mechanical setup verification

init-pipeline Step 4 now runs a deterministic bash script instead of
AI-interpreted checks. verify-setup.sh validates pipeline files, hook
wiring, and toolchain commands. /doctor wraps the script with AI-level
diagnostics for config accuracy and rule completeness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(todo): add TODO-007f CF + Custom auth plan for admin panel (#184)

* feat(admin-panel): Wave 1 — complete project scaffold for React admin panel

Initializes the admin-panel/ SPA at the monorepo root. This is the
foundation layer (TODO-007a, Day 1) for panel.zyntapos.com.

Includes:
- Vite 6 + React 19 + TypeScript 5 (strict mode) project config
- TanStack Router v1 (file-based, type-safe) + TanStack Query v5
- Tailwind CSS 4 with ZyntaPOS brand tokens (dark admin theme)
- shadcn/ui components.json + Inter/JetBrains Mono fonts
- ky HTTP client with CF Access JWT auth + admin token management
- TanStack Query client (30s stale, retry 2, window-focus refetch)
- All 9 TypeScript domain interfaces (license, store, user, audit,
  sync, metrics, config, alert, api pagination)
- Utility functions: formatCurrency(LKR), maskLicenseKey, formatBytes,
  buildQueryString, formatRelativeTime
- CSV/JSON export helpers (PapaParse)
- Vitest + RTL + MSW test infrastructure (80% coverage thresholds)
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (node:22-alpine → nginx:alpine, port 3000)
- nginx.conf: SPA fallback, gzip, security headers, 1y asset caching
- .gitignore, .nvmrc (Node 22), .env.example, .prettierrc, eslint.config.js
- TanStack Router root layout stub + Dashboard route stub

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* feat(admin-panel): Wave 2 partial — layout shell, hooks, stores (mobile-first)

Adds responsive layout foundation:
- AppShell: mobile bottom nav + tablet icon sidebar + desktop full sidebar
- useAuth: CF Access JWT decode + RBAC permission check + dev fallback
- useDebounce, useMediaQuery: utility hooks (useIsMobile/Tablet/Desktop)
- ui-store: Zustand sidebar state + toast queue + persist
- filter-store: Zustand persisted filter state per page

Remaining layout components (Sidebar, Header, MobileBottomNav,
shared components) to follow in next commit.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* feat(admin-panel): Wave 2 complete — mobile-first responsive layout shell

Full responsive layout with 3 breakpoints:
- Mobile (<768px): bottom nav bar (4 tabs + More), hamburger header,
  slide-in full sidebar with backdrop overlay
- Tablet (768-1023px): icon-only collapsed sidebar (56px)
- Desktop (>=1024px): full sidebar (240px) + collapse toggle

Components:
- Sidebar: collapsible nav with 4 groups (10 routes), active indicators
- Header: mobile hamburger + breadcrumbs (desktop) + notifications + user menu
- MobileBottomNav: fixed bottom, 4 primary tabs + More button
- Breadcrumbs: auto-generated from route hierarchy
- UserMenu: CF Access JWT display, role badge, sign out
- StatusBadge: color-coded for 17 status variants
- ToastContainer: auto-dismiss toasts, positioned above bottom nav on mobile
- LoadingState: Skeleton, CardSkeleton, TableSkeleton, PageLoader
- EmptyState, ErrorBoundary (with retry)

All touch targets >=44px. Root layout wired to AppShell.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* feat(admin-panel): Wave 3A — Dashboard page, charts, KPI cards, metrics API

Dashboard page (routes/index.tsx):
- Period selector (Today/Week/Month) with auto-refresh every 30s
- 4 KPI cards: Total Stores, Active Licenses, Revenue Today, Sync Health
- Responsive grids: 1→2→4 cols for KPIs, 1→2 cols for charts
- Revenue trend (AreaChart), Store comparison (BarChart)
- License edition distribution (PieChart), Service uptime (horizontal BarChart)
- Recent alerts panel with StatusBadge

Charts (src/components/charts/):
- SalesChart: gradient AreaChart, custom tooltip, XAxis formatted by granularity
- StoreComparisonChart: multi-color BarChart with store-level tooltip
- LicenseDistribution: donut PieChart with edition color coding
- UptimeChart: horizontal BarChart, color-coded green/amber/red by %

Shared:
- KpiCard: icon, value, trend arrow (TrendingUp/Down), skeleton loading

API:
- useDashboardKPIs, useSalesChart, useStoreComparison (30s refetch)
- useSalesReport, useProductPerformance (reports page reuse)

All mobile-first responsive. Charts use ResponsiveContainer.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* fix(ci): lowercase Docker image tag — GHCR requires lowercase repository names

${{ github.repository }} includes 'ZyntaPOS-KMM' which is uppercase.
Added a step to convert to lowercase before building/tagging the image.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* feat(deploy): activate admin panel on panel.zyntapos.com

- Add admin-panel service to docker-compose.yml (ghcr image, port 3000)
- Update Caddyfile to route panel.zyntapos.com → admin-panel:3000

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* chore: add VPS ad-hoc workflow for diagnostics

* fix(deploy): use /health endpoint for admin-panel healthcheck

nginx.conf exposes /health at port 3000; using the dedicated endpoint
avoids wget exit-code issues when fetching the SPA root.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* fix(deploy): use 127.0.0.1 in nginx container healthchecks

Alpine Linux resolves 'localhost' to ::1 (IPv6) but nginx only
listens on IPv4. Using 127.0.0.1 fixes healthcheck for:
  - canary (also use /ping endpoint instead of / for explicit 200)
  - admin-panel
  - website (use / instead of /ping which Astro doesn't serve)

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* fix(ci): align admin-panel VITE env var names with React source

Build step and Docker build-args used VITE_API_BASE_URL / VITE_LICENSE_BASE_URL /
VITE_SYNC_BASE_URL but the React source and Dockerfile ARGs expect VITE_API_URL /
VITE_LICENSE_URL / VITE_SYNC_URL. Mismatched names caused the env vars to be silently
ignored during Docker image build, falling back to Dockerfile defaults.

Also corrects the build-args values to use production URLs (matching Dockerfile
defaults) for consistency.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* ci(admin-panel): add Docker image build to CI Gate deploy chain

The admin-panel image (panel.zyntapos.com) was missing from ci-gate.yml,
causing the VPS to run without a valid GHCR image for the admin-panel
container. Added build-admin-panel-image job and included it in
trigger-deploy needs so the deploy only fires after all images are ready.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013es7eQ6g88H4yhqUHzNyUw

* docs(todo): add TODO-007f CF + Custom auth plan for admin panel

7-day implementation plan for replacing Cloudflare Access-only auth
(Option 1) with a hybrid CF + Custom approach:
- Cloudflare: DDoS, WAF, TLS (kept as-is, orange-cloud proxy)
- Custom: ZyntaPOS-branded login, backend JWT admin auth API,
  brute-force lockout, TOTP MFA, Google SSO (@zyntasolutions.com),
  admin user management UI, full auth audit trail

Plan covers:
- Day 1: Ktor admin auth routes + PostgreSQL schema (admin_users,
  admin_sessions, admin_mfa_backup_codes) + JWT issuance
- Day 2: React login UI + Zustand auth store (replaces CF cookie)
- Day 3: Security hardening (headers, CSRF, rate limiting, lockout)
- Day 4: TOTP MFA (setup wizard, QR code, backup codes)
- Day 5: Google OAuth 2.0 PKCE SSO (domain-restricted)
- Day 6: Admin user management CRUD UI
- Day 7: Tests, CF Access bypass migration, CI update

No paid auth services required. All self-hosted in existing Ktor backend.
Updated 000-execution-plan.md to include 007f in Phase 2 TODO table.

commit.message:
fix build: remove misplaced verify-email page from api directory

Page was accidentally placed at src/app/api/verify-email/page.jsx;
correct location src/app/verify-email/page.jsx already exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🤖 Update Windows ISO links - 2026-03-07 20:34:12 UTC

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove test mode - send daily email to all subscribers

Changes:
- Removed test mode that limited emails to Nalin only
- Now sends to all waitlist subscribers (respecting unsubscribe status)
- Added force_resend query param to bypass idempotency check
- Updated success message to show actual count
- Each subscriber gets individual email with unique unsubscribe link

This enables immediate send to all subscribers while maintaining:
- P0 security (auth required)
- Individual emails (no email exposure)
- Unsubscribe functionality
- Idempotency protection (unless force_resend=true)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Fly.io deployment config with Docker and SQLite volume

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Hebrew/Arabic translation toggle to all study modes

- Add local translationLang state to each mode (not using global language context)
- FlashcardMode: Toggle button in header, example sentences stay in English
- QuizMode: Toggle in intro screen, questions regenerate when language changes
- MatchingMode: Toggle in intro screen, column header updates dynamically
- SpellingMode: Toggle in intro screen, translation hint updates
- WordScrambleMode: Toggle in intro screen, word hint updates

Students can now switch between Hebrew (עברית) and Arabic (عربي) translations
while keeping example sentences in English only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pwa): replace icons with new Lighthouse logo - all PWA sizes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build 122 — fix cold launch: save nag_id in didReceive when pushService nil

launchOptions[.remoteNotification] is not reliably populated when
UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate is active. Instead, detect cold launch
in didReceive by checking if pushService is nil and save the nag_id
directly to UserDefaults — restorePendingNag() picks it up on first appear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: hard reject rules in conviction filter — D/E >5x and negative op margin

Lynch/Graham value principles: high leverage inflates ROE artificially (ENR 24x D/E),
negative operating margin means core business loses money (LYFT -11.2%).
Both now hard-rejected with grade D regardless of other metrics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove checkerboard background from sheep sprites (#387)

## Summary
- Source spritesheets had AI-generated fake transparency checkerboard
baked into pixel data (~243 gray alternating with ~254 white)
- Background removal threshold (BG_MIN=248) only caught white squares,
leaving gray grid visible in-game
- Lowered BG_MIN to 235 and added post-resize LANCZOS halo cleanup to
`split_sheep_sprites.py`
- Re-processed all 40 sheep sprite frames to remove background artifacts

## Test plan
- [ ] Launch game, observe sheep sprites have clean transparent
backgrounds (no visible grid/squares)
- [ ] Verify sheep idle and walk animations display correctly in all 8
directions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Signed-off-by: Dave Tashner <davetashner@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Dave Tashner <davetashner@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Feature: AI agent - Conversational scrutinio data entry (Phase 1)

Add ability for the AI agent to read and write scrutinio (ballot counting)
data through natural conversation with confirmation before saving.

New AI tools:
- get_scrutinio_status: retrieve current data for a section
- save_scrutinio_data: partial update with optimistic locking and audit trail

Implementation:
- tools.py: scrutinio_data_tool with get/save function declarations
- views.py: _handle_get_scrutinio_status, _handle_save_scrutinio_data handlers
  with permission checks (can_enter_section_data), SectionDataHistory tracking,
  pessimistic locking (select_for_update), scheda matching by name/number
- views.py: scrutinio data included in user profile context (current values
  per section + schede, so AI can show old->new)
- settings.py: GESTIONE DATI SCRUTINIO prompt section with semantic
  interpretation protocol, confirmation requirement, partial update rules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace date inputs with visual month calendar picker

1C-style month grid: 3 years side by side, click to select range,
highlighted months, quick period links, year navigation arrows.
Backend unchanged - still uses period=custom&date_from&date_to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: qrservice
repository.description: QR Code Service - Django web application

commit.message:
Phase 2: matrix-free LSQR operators + forward solver validation

solver/validate_forward.py  — applies uniform 20 GPa on culet_sample, checks
  D_g > 0 (sign convention), linearity (ratio = 2.000 PASS), magnitudes in range

solver/operators.py   — NVOperator class with matrix-free matvec (A@c) and
  rmatvec (A^T@r) for LSQR:
  • matvec:  pixel tractions -> load vector -> MUMPS solve -> stress eval -> D_g
  • rmatvec: residual -> M_g^T (=M_g, symmetric) -> adjoint traction (S@n_culet)
             -> MUMPS solve -> read displacement at pixels
  • check_adjoint(): finite-difference consistency test <Ac,r> == <c,A^Tr>
  • as_linear_operator(): wraps for scipy.sparse.linalg

solver/invert.py — run_lsqr(): Tikhonov-augmented LSQR via AugmentedOperator
  [A; sqrt(lam)*I] @ c = [d; 0]; reconstruct_final_stress() (single solve);
  run_synthetic_test(): Gaussian traction -> D_g -> invert -> recovery check;
  load_nv_data() helper

solver/forward.py — 3 API fixes for DOLFINx 0.10:
  • gmshio import: dolfinx.io.gmsh (was dolfinx.io.gmshio)
  • read_from_msh returns MeshData namedtuple (not 3-tuple)
  • Function.vector -> Function.x.petsc_vec
  • interpolation_points: property not method
  • eval() shape normalised: np.reshape(val, (-1, 9)) handles both (9,) and (M,9)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

SEO: Add validation script + v3.0 changelog

- Add SEO health check script (npx tsx scripts/validate-seo.ts) that validates
  JSON-LD schemas, meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt, RSS feed, and OG images
- Add v3.0 changelog entry documenting domain launch and latest improvements
- Note: GitHub Actions deploy workflow saved locally (.github/workflows/deploy.yml)
  — requires PAT with workflow scope to push

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.0.0: Security hardening, code review fixes, and end-user README

Server:
- Parse replays outside SQLite transaction to avoid holding write lock
- Add timing-safe auth token comparison (crypto.timingSafeEqual)
- Add temp file cleanup on all upload code paths
- Use crypto.randomBytes for temp file names
- Validate filenames on multipart upload route
- Decode percent-encoded X-Filename header
- Add requirePlayer() helper with 400 response
- Wrap WebSocket JSON.parse in try/catch
- Add runInTransaction() with batch processing
- Fix limit parameter NaN handling
- Upgrade Docker to Node 24, add non-root user
- Add replays volume to docker-compose
- Add Swagger API documentation
- Update npm dependencies

Client:
- Remove reqwest dependency, use ureq for connectivity checks
- Disable http_status_as_error to preserve response bodies on 4xx/5xx
- Remove unused notify-debouncer-full dependency
- Combine duplicate/server-connected lock acquisitions (TOCTOU fix)
- Remove redundant server_connected writes in event handler
- Clone bulk_progress before painting to avoid holding mutex
- Use try_send() instead of async send() for watcher stop
- Cap exponential backoff at 64 seconds
- UTF-8 safe response body logging
- Percent-encode non-ASCII filenames in upload header
- Settings save returns Result with error handling
- Tray thread breaks on channel close
- Add scanning guard flag to prevent concurrent scans
- Version 1.0.0

commit.message:
v2.6.13: Revert to SUN2000-10KTL (recognized), pv_w at both 32064+32080

Nibe always reads 32080 for PV regardless of profile. With MAP0 model
strings (hyphens or underscores) Nibe never recognizes the device, so
smart meter and battery are lost.

SUN2000-10KTL is recognized → battery + smart meter work.
Write pv_w to both 32080 (Nibe KTL reads this) and 32064 (MAP0 spec
DC input, for future compatibility if Nibe adds MAP0 support).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(bot): три исправления логики живого бота

1. QUIET_TRANSITION → Grid вместо HOLD (market_regime.py)
   ADX 22-32, ATR<2% — боковое движение после тренда.
   Grid идеален для этого состояния. Ранее бот ничего не делал.

2. TF убран из BEAR_TREND (strategy_selector.py)
   В нисходящем тренде TF не входит в сделки (нет long-сигналов).
   BEAR_TREND → DCA только, weight=1.0.

3. SMC graceful_transition: active_positions → _positions (bot_orchestrator.py)
   hasattr(adapter, 'active_positions') всегда возвращал False —
   атрибута не существует у SMCStrategyAdapter.
   Позиции теперь закрываются через adapter._positions.items().

Tests: обновлены 3 теста под новое поведение, 1230 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove broken offset pagination from listAgents, use cursor-based only (bd-nped) (#23)

listAgents() accepted an offset parameter but applied it as a client-side
slice after fetching from the Letta API. When limit was also passed, the API
returned only N agents, then slice(offset, offset+limit) returned empty.

Fix: Remove offset from listAgents signature and all callers. The Letta API
uses cursor-based pagination (after/before/limit) which is already correctly
wired through. Callers that need pagination should use after/before cursors.

commit.message:
Add roof plan scale-based area measurement with cross-check

Primary change: Roof plans now get a dedicated area measurement pass
that uses the detected scale to measure the building footprint, in
addition to the existing item-counting extraction (drains, pipes, etc.).

New features:
- ROOF_PLAN_AREA_MEASUREMENT_PROMPT: detailed GPT-4o prompt that
  instructs the AI to measure the building outline using the scale bar
  or dimension lines, supporting rectangular, L-shaped, and irregular
  buildings
- measure_roof_plan_area(): dedicated function for roof plan area
  extraction using 'high' detail mode for precision
- pixel_based_area_estimate(): validates measurements against image
  pixel dimensions using known DPI and scale ratio
- cross_check_roof_area(): compares roof area from multiple sources
  (roof plan vs floor plan/slab plan), flags discrepancies, and
  selects the most reliable value

Pipeline changes:
- Roof plan pages now run dual extraction: items + area measurement
- Floor/slab plan measurements tagged with source for cross-checking
- Added opencv-python-headless to requirements for future pixel analysis

commit.message:
docs(playwright): update CLAUDE-REFERENCE for suite_completed auto-kill and stdout_snippet (#30)

* feat(playwright): auto-kill demo process on suite_completed in check_demo_result

Adds suite_completed tracking to DemoProgress and auto-kills the demo
process in checkDemoResult() when the suite has fully finished, ensuring
no orphaned demo processes linger after Playwright suites complete.

Also fixes pre-existing stdout_snippet divergence in inlined readDemoProgress
test helper, and adds 16 new tests covering suite_completed behavior and
auto-kill status determination.

commit.message:
Refresh ticker on category switch (#28)

* Add action figure category support with sidebar switcher

Introduces a category system (Transformers / Action Figures) so collectors
of non-transforming figures (GI Joe, Star Wars, He-Man, etc.) get their
own filtered experience. Sidebar toggle switches between categories;
all views, leaderboards, market pulse, and stats filter by active category.

- DB migration adds category column to Figures table
- Category switcher in sidebar with collapsed abbreviations (TF/AF)
- Scale-based class tiers for action figures (3.75", 6", 7", 12")
- Opt-in "Has Transformation?" checkbox on submission/scorecard forms
  (default checked for Transformers, unchecked for Action Figures)
- Dynamic score formula: /90 with transformation, /70 without
- All 16 API endpoints filter by ?category= query param
- All frontend fetches pass active category

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump styles.css version to bust cache for category switcher

The category switcher CSS wasn't loading because styles.css cache
version wasn't bumped with the other files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix category switcher crushed by flex shrink in sidebar

The sidebar is a flex column and when content exceeded viewport height,
the category-switcher was shrinking to 1.8px due to default flex-shrink.
Adding flex-shrink: 0 prevents it from collapsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Filter brand dropdown by active category

When in Action Figures mode, brand dropdown only shows brands that
have existing action figures (empty for new category). Transformer
approved brands only appear in Transformer mode. Users can always
add new brands via "Other" option.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update documentation and README for category system

- Add new "Categories" section (02) to in-app docs explaining the
  Transformers/Action Figures switcher, size tiers, and transformation opt-in
- Update Platform Overview to mention both categories
- Update Trade Scan section: physical quality now shows 7 core + 2 optional
  transformation attributes
- Update Grading System: dynamic approval score formula (/90 vs /70)
- Renumber all doc sections (02-17 → 03-18)
- Add Categories & Scoring section to README.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refresh ticker tape when switching categories

The ticker wasn't updating on category switch because startTicker()
was only called on initial page load. Now it re-runs when the user
toggles between Transformers and Action Figures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Jonathan Williams <dubbs@Jonathans-MacBook-Pro.local>

Configure env-based API URL and CORS for Vercel + Railway deployment

- Frontend API base URL reads from VITE_API_URL env var (falls back to localhost)
- Backend CORS origin reads from FRONTEND_URL env var (falls back to localhost)
- Backend PORT reads from PORT env var (Railway sets this automatically)

commit.message:
Implement poison/disease/insanity mechanics (§1.6.9)

Add osric_afflictions.ttrpg with three affliction systems:
- Poison: save vs DeathParalysisPoison, lethal or damage-only (save for half)
- Disease: contraction check, 2d8 incubation/duration, stat/roll penalties,
  fatality detection when any d8 shows 8
- Insanity: d6 table with 6 condition types (ability decline, amnesia,
  divergent personality, disinterest, pathological liar, phobia)

17 integration tests covering all mechanics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(build): close three post-review gaps in Phase 9.4 (#26)

1. Hook policy now requires *explicit* exclusions for all non-hook platforms.
   Previously: only errored if the platform key existed with the wrong mode.
   Now: errors if any of [claude-web, claude-ios, cursor, codex] is absent
   or doesn't have mode: excluded. All existing hook skills already comply.

2. load-platforms.mjs missing-directory is now a fatal error (errors.push)
   rather than a console.warn. compile.mjs already propagates loadErrors to
   fatalErrors → exit 1, so no compiler change was needed.

3. Add node:test fixture suite (18 tests, 0 failures):
   - scripts/build/test/policy.test.mjs: unit tests for validateSkillPolicy,
     validatePlatformPolicy, and loadPlatforms with synthetic fixtures
   - scripts/build/test/compile.integration.test.mjs: spawns the real compiler
     against shared/skills/ and asserts clean exit + no [error] lines
   - package.json: add "test" script (node --test scripts/build/test/*.test.mjs)

4. PLAN.md: remove duplicated Phase 9.4 heading from rapid iteration; merge
   the two consecutive ## Phase 9.4 blocks into one with updated status line.
